insight: In a Tatsu Nullifier set, the dd dd dde opening (as P1) is a deliberate choice to play around expected aggression: the player believed dd dde into Centurion would lose to Tatsus, but that after the opponent commits to heavy green, Centurion becomes the winning line.
author: amalloy
context: Discussion of xyotsuba's unconventional dd dd dde opening in a high-level game against mrguy888.

insight: xyotsuba confirmed his plan in the dd dd dde game was dd dd daa into Tatsu with Blood Pact and Corpus for support, which he acknowledged was sketchy against triple Conduit for cluster rush.
author: xyotsuba
context: xyotsuba clarified his own strategic intent in the discussed game.

insight: Shredder's value is determined by the opponent's absorb threshold: it has 4 HP regardless of whether the opponent is dealing absorb+4 or absorb+10 damage, meaning it does not gain additional value against higher damage totals beyond the absorb barrier.
author: mrguy888
context: velizar_ asked whether frontline units become better late game when opponent damage exceeds absorb capacity by a large margin.

insight: The Master Bot can execute Iso Kronus + Thorium Dynamo + Gauss Cannon strategies effectively enough to defeat experienced players, suggesting this combination is a strong bot line rather than a typical bot weakness.
author: velizar_
context: velizar_ reported losing multiple times to the Master Bot using Iso Kronus + Thorium Dynamo + Gauss Cannon, with a second player also reporting losses to this strategy.

insight: In base set + Zemora Voidbringer, the consensus opening is Fast Conduit for P1 and Natural Conduit for P2; ignoring Zemora and racing with Tarsiers, Walls, and Rhinos is also a viable counter, forcing the Zemora player into less efficient Walls and Gauss Cannons.
author: deadhour
context: Discussion of best openings in a base + Zemora Voidbringer set.

insight: The value of 1 damage next turn relative to 2 gold depends on the soak unit's gold-equivalent cost: Wall has a block value (BV) of ~2.22, meaning 1 damage is worth slightly more than 2 gold in most sets. Exceptions include Aegis (BV=2), Polywall (BV=1.95), and Plexo Cell (BV=1.92), which are at or below 2.
author: megasupp
context: Discussion of whether 1 damage is worth approximately 2 gold and how to value Steelsplitter vs Tarsier decisions.

insight: Holding a Scorchilla for one turn costs approximately 1 attack worth of value; holding it for multiple turns loses proportionally more, so holding Scorchilla is only correct if the value gained (e.g., absorbing on it or abusing enemy defense) equals or exceeds that lost attack.
author: consensus
context: stacko asked about the rule of thumb for holding Scorchilla, confirmed by velizar_.

insight: In a Deadeye Operative mirror, P2's standard build is DD DD float DBB, then buy out all Deadeyes and Drones before much else.
author: lyra1712
context: Discussion of how to play a Deadeye Operative set when a mirror is predicted.

insight: In sets with Energy Matrix, fast efficient attackers like Hellhound are less effective than normal because the powerful absorb neutralizes their speed advantage; slower attack that can sync past the absorb barrier is preferable.
author: deadhour
context: Post-game analysis of a player who over-invested in Hellhounds in a set with Energy Matrix.

insight: When breaching, the correct target priority is generally: kill the most efficient unit by value (e.g., Vivid Drone > Tarsier > Drone > Steelsplitter in some contexts), but a Tarsier that can kill a Vivid Drone is worth far more than an average Tarsier, making Tarsier often the correct kill in practice over Vivid Drone.
author: mrguy888
context: Extended discussion on breach target priority, with amalloy and mrguy888 refining the community's understanding.

insight: 3 gold is only worth more than 1 damage when that damage is killing units at or below blocker-level health; damage that removes units above blocker HP can be worth far more than 3 gold.
author: mrguy888
context: Discussion of breach target priority comparing Vivid Drone vs Tarsier kills.

insight: Thorium Dynamo is secretly less breach-proof than its HP/gold ratio suggests, because it operates at full efficiency until fully destroyed; thus it is correct to defend Thorium Dynamos even when all your attackers are breach-proof.
author: mrguy888
context: Discussion of whether Thorium Dynamo is breach-proof and whether defending it is correct.

insight: Going temporarily breach-proof with Thorium Dynamo while incoming damage is low, then switching back to defending, is a valid strategy but competes directly with absorbing during low-damage windows, making it more situational than it initially appears.
author: mrguy888
context: stacko asked about temporarily going breach-proof with Thorium Dynamo; mrguy888 identified the key tradeoff.

insight: Centurion is a much better purchase than Arms Race in most situations; Blood Pact used defensively is considered strong ("op"), and the 1-Conduit opening is evaluated as a mistake at high levels.
author: mrguy888
context: mrguy888 reviewed a game and identified double Arms Race and 1-Conduit opening as the main mistakes.

insight: Steelsplitter is considered one of the worst base-set attackers: it costs more than Tarsier (approximately 2.67 more gold-equivalent), cannot attack and defend simultaneously, and is only correct to buy when you have surplus blue resources and no better option. Rhino is considered worse than Steelsplitter but both are generally poor units.
author: mrguy888
context: Extended community debate on whether Steelsplitter is underrated; mrguy888 and others concluded it is legitimately bad compared to Tarsier.

insight: DE DDE CC is a very poor opening; in high-economy sets with better attackers than Gauss Cannon, buying an Engineer instead of a Drone on turn 1 (when you could afford a Drone) is a mistake because Engineers' purpose in early game is to enable more Drone purchases.
author: amalloy
context: New player asked how to play a set correctly; amalloy and mrguy888 explained why the DE DDE CC opening is suboptimal.

insight: P2 can do either DE DDD or DD DDE and buy the same number of Drones total; the difference is getting one Drone one turn sooner with the latter, worth approximately 1 free gold. Buying an Engineer instead of a Drone on turn 1 contradicts the purpose of buying Engineers to accelerate Drone production.
author: mrguy888
context: mrguy888 explained the logical relationship between Engineer and Drone purchases to a new player.

insight: The fastest reasonable P1 Odin opening line is dd dde ddd dddb dbb 1.
author: amalloy
context: amalloy stated the fastest reasonable P1 Odin line in response to a question about it.

insight: In a green-red mirror, the player with the smaller economy and more attackers has the advantage. The correct response to an opponent going green-red is to build a big absorber (e.g., Xaetron or Defense Grid) rather than mirroring the aggression.
author: masn6811
context: masn6811 analyzed a game where both players went green-red and explained why the mirror favors the aggressor.

insight: P2 Natural Conduit's power lies specifically in denying P1 the ability to go Conduit first; if you are not spending the green resources, there is no point in buying the Conduit early as P2.
author: mrguy888
context: Discussion of whether Natural Conduit as P2 was correct in a set where green was not being spent efficiently.

insight: When breaching against Thorium Dynamo, it is normally the highest priority target in breach-proof scenarios because it provides full value until destroyed and wounding it without killing it grants no immediate benefit.
author: velizar_
context: velizar_ explained breach target priority to a new player asking which units to kill first during a breach.
